key_findings: Cryo-EM structures of 5-HT2AR with various psychedelic ligands reveal common and distinct molecular interaction motifs among different chemotypes, advancing the understanding of 5-HT2A activation.
A remarkable global structural homology was observed across tryptamines, ergolines, and phenethylamines at the 5-HT2AR receptor level, despite their chemical diversity.

Distinct ligand-stabilized conformational transitions were identified, including a novel non-canonical (NC) state stabilized by the β-arrestin-biased agonist RS130-180, providing insights into mechanisms of arrestin bias.

evidence.quote_or_path: FULL PAPER.Page 1: "all classical psychedelics are 5-HT2A serotonin (5-hydroxytryptamine; 5-HT) receptor (5-HT2AR) agonists"; FULL PAPER.Page 2: "we obtained seven cryo-EM structures of the 5-HT2AR with representative compounds from each chemical class, including 5-HT, psilocin, DMT, mescaline, BOL, LSD, and a β-arrestin 2 (β-arr2) biased compound RS130-180 (Fig. 1)."
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly states classical psychedelics like DMT are 5-HT2AR agonists and includes DMT in the list of agonists studied in this work.
The PDB title indicates a complex from which this structure was refined, and the paper explicitly states this structure is one of seven 'active state' cryo-EM structures.
evidence.quote_or_path: PDB METADATA.structure_details.title: Local refinement of 5-HT2AR bound to DMT in complex with a mini-Gq protein and scFv16 obtained by cryo-electron microscopy (cryoEM); FULL PAPER.Page 3.Results.Structural elucidation of psychedelic drug interactions: "we determined seven active state cryo-EM structures of the agonist-bound 5-HT2AR in complex with heterotrimeric Gq."
